content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA                                    EDMUND G. BROWN JR., Governor

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
100 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(916) 322-5660


                                                January 23, 1976


Mr. Leonard Panish
Department of Registrar - Recorder
808 North Spring
Los Angeles, CA  90012


Dear Mr. Panish:

     Thank you for your letter of January 14, 1976,
commenting on the Commission's recently adopted opinion
in response to a request by Mr. Rundstrom of Yolo County
(No. 75-084, Dec. 3, 1975). In the two hearings prior
to adoption of that opinion, the Commission con-
sidered the policy arguments you have suggested with re-
spect to the need for prompt disclosure and timely
notification of filers, but concluded that, these policy
arguments, notwithstanding, the intent and language of
the Act requires the result set forth in the adopted
opinion.

     A pre-notification plan which attempts to remind each
filer of approaching deadlines is laudable, and nothing
in the Political Reform Act precludes implementation of
such a plan. However, the Rundstrom opinion makes it
clear that such a pre-notification cannot be used to
invoke mandatory penalties whenever a filing deadline is
violated. The purpose in amending the Act was to pro-
vide filing officers with discretion in certain limited
circumstances. If a filing officer were permitted to
negate that discretion by sending a notice to the filer
prior to the deadline, the original grant of discretion
would be an illusion. I understand and sympathize with
the concerns voiced in your letter, but the clear intent
of the Act is counter to them.

     If you have any further questions, please feel free
to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Ted Prim,
TP:pvp                                  Staff Counsel

COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES
DEPARTMENT OF REGISTRAR—RECORDER
808 NORTH SPRING, LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA, 90012 / 974-6701

LEONARD PANISH
REGISTRAR-RECORDER

CHARLES L. CAMPBELL, JR.
CHIEF DEPUTY

January 14, 1976

Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Suite 522
Sacramento, California 95814

COMMISSION OPINION #75-084

Gentlemen and Mesdames:

I would like to express my concerns about portions of the subject opinion
which deal with the discretionary authority of a filing officer in assessing late penalties.

The final paragraph of your opinion states that the purpose of the notice
of the filing requirements is to inform persons that they are subject to
penalty because the deadline for filing reports has passed.

I am concerned about this interpretation, as I feel that it is not consistent
with the purposes of the Act for the following reasons:

-- The purposes of the Act as expressed in Section 81001 (a)
require full and prompt disclosure of campaign receipts
and expenditures in order that voters may be fully informed and improper practices may be inhibited. The statements required before an election, therefore, would be
considered especially important because the filing deadlines of twelve or seven days prior to an election permit
the public an opportunity for access to information concerning contributions and expenditures prior to the election
itself. If a specific notice of the filing requirement must
be sent after the deadline, the concept of public access to
essential information on a timely basis becomes meaningless.

-- It is my belief that the Act also assures each filer of his
right to receive full and adequate notice of the obligation the
law places on him. I believe, therefore, that penalties are
mandatory only after the filer has been provided, on a timely

- 2 -

basis, with all the information and materials needed to
accomplish a filing in accordance with the law.

By furnishing an information notice prior to the stated
deadlines I feel the integrity of the Act is preserved and
the rights of all filers are respected.

Without going into detail I would like to state that Los Angeles County
has implemented a comprehensive prenotification system to assure that
filers are aware of their responsibilities under the law, and to provide
them with general and specific information and reporting forms to facili-
tate compliance.

In our judgment our system safeguards the rights of the filer and also ac-
complishes the purposes of the Act by providing the public with information
by the specified deadlines. While your opinion safeguards the rights
of the filer, it appears to be at the expense of the public's "right to know"
prior to the time an election occurs.

I am requesting your approval, therefore, of the Los Angeles County
system of prenotification of filing requirements which is tied to the
legal dates that campaign reports are due.

I appreciate your consideration.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

LEONARD PANISH
Registrar-Recorder
